judgment.summary: Zscaler shows strong revenue growth and healthy free cash flow, but is currently unprofitable on an operating and net basis with a high forward P/E.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported quarter (Jan 2026) EPS of $1.01 beat estimates of $0.90 by 12.6%. Next earnings date is May 26, 2026.
positives: Revenue growth of 25.9% YoY indicates strong top-line expansion.
Free cash flow of $1.02B TTM demonstrates robust cash generation.
Gross margin of 76.6% is very healthy.
Current ratio of 1.90 and quick ratio of 1.75 suggest good short-term liquidity.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target price of $224.27, suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Operating margin of -6.2% and profit margin of -2.3% indicate the company is not currently profitable.
Return on equity of -3.6% and return on assets of -1.4% show poor returns on capital.
Forward P/E of 37.67 suggests the stock is priced for significant future growth.
Debt/Equity ratio of 85.00 indicates a notable level of leverage.

news.paragraph: Zscaler shares have seen a significant rebound, with recent news highlighting its strategic initiatives in enterprise AI security through Project AI Guardian and partnerships with Google Cloud and global system integrators. This focus on AI adoption and security solutions, coupled with a general software stock rebound, suggests positive momentum. Traders may also be positioning ahead of May 26, though the broader cybersecurity sector is facing some scrutiny.
